Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statement is correct for vane shear test?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Limitations of vane shear test:
• There is no mechanism to measure pore water pressure.
• It is not suitable for sand and dry soils.
• Drainage can’t be controlled.

Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A 10 m high smooth retaining wall with a vertical face, retains a cohesive backfill having an unconfined compressive strength of 50 kN/m2 and unit weight is 19.5 kN/m3. The overturning moment due to active earth pressure of back fill is ______ kN-m.

(Round off to nearest integer) Assume: Tension cracks are developed.

NAT
Correct answer: 1338
Solution

1338 (1335 - 1340)

(Neglect the rotation of wall). (Take γw = 9.81 kN/m3)

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A consolidated undrained triaxial test conducted on a clay has a minor principal stress of 175 kN/m2 and deviator stress at failure is 225 kN/m2. The shear stress at failure plane is _____kN/m2. (Correct upto two decimal places)
Assume soil is cohesionless.

NAT
Correct answer: 103.53
Solution

103.53 (103 - 104)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A vertical excavation was made in a clay having unit weight of 20 kN/m3. Maximum depth of the unsupported vertical cut to be made in this clay is 3.5 m. If this clay is used as a backfill against a retaining wall upto a height of 7.5 m, then the total active earth pressure is (in kN per meter length of wall)
Assume: Angle of internal friction

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

NAT
Correct answer: 26.64
Solution

26.64 (26.40 - 26.90)

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c)

(Correct upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 10.50
Solution

10.50 (10 - 11)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is not the assumption of Rankine’s earth pressure theory?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Rankine (1857) considered the equilibrium of soil element within a soil mass bounded by a plane
surface.
The following assumptions are made in Rankine’s earth pressure theory:
• The soil mass is homogeneous and semi-infinite.
• The soil is dry and cohesionless.
• The ground surface is plane, which may be horizontal or inclined.
• The back of the retaining wall is smooth and vertical.
• The soil element is in a state of plastic equilibrium.

NAT
Correct answer: 49.06
Solution

49.06 (48.5 - 50.5)
